title: The 26 Most Anticipated Movies of 2026
format: Back from the holidays, Sean and Amanda check the box office (Avatar past $1B, a Sydney Sweeney trash hit) and the pre-Globes awards race, then count down their most-anticipated films of 2026: 13 apiece, best last. Reheated favorites, auteur horror, a Robert Pattinson year, and an honorable-mentions list covering international titles, the horror wave, rom-coms, and the year's big IP.

segments.heading: Back from break
segments.note: the holiday in a few movies

films.title: Marty Supreme
films.note: The film both hosts discussed most over the break, a genuine flashpoint that broke A24 containment the way 'Sinners' and 'One Battle After Another' did. People wanted to relitigate it for 30 minutes at parties; the dog got more outrage than the ending.

films.title: If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
films.note: Amanda's pick for the film that best described her break, cited after she had a full AI meltdown at a CVS phone tree trying to get her kid antibiotics on New Year's Eve. 'Thank you to Mary Bronstein for seeing Amanda this year.'

films.title: The Big Lebowski
films.note: Sean's answer for the movie that best described his break: floating through life, not worried about a paycheck, hanging with loved ones. And now, welcome back to hell.

segments.heading: Holiday box office
segments.note: Avatar past a billion, Disney's year, and a Sydney Sweeney trash hit

films.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
films.note: Over $1B in 18 days, $770M international, on pace for maybe 1.6-1.7B. If it gets there, four of the ten highest-grossing films worldwide are James Cameron's. Filmmakers (Michael Mann among them) keep naming it a favorite; the hosts found it a touch iterative next to the second one.

films.title: Zootopia 2
films.note: $1.6B after six weekends, now the No. 3 animated release ever, past 'Frozen II.' Part of a monster Disney year alongside 'Lilo & Stitch,' three Marvel movies, and 'Fire and Ash.'

films.title: Inside Out 2
films.note: Cited as the No. 2 animated film of all time.

films.title: Ne Zha 2
films.note: The No. 1 animated film worldwide, primarily on overseas money. Sean started it on HBO Max and turned it off, missing the references.

films.title: The Housemaid
films.note: $92M worldwide in three weekends, bigger than 'Marty.' A Sydney Sweeney beach-read adaptation the hosts think isn't good but that proves the Colleen Hoover blueprint: adapt a popular book with hot young people. Amanda had an okay time; Sean had notes.

films.title: Marty Supreme
films.note: $56M in nine days domestically before international, already A24's No. 3 all-time behind 'Everything Everywhere' and 'Civil War.' Chalamet sold the fuck out of a two-and-a-half-hour period piece.

films.title: Anaconda
films.note: Among the holiday releases the show covered.

films.title: Song Sung Blue
films.note: Among the holiday releases the show covered.

films.title: Regretting You
films.note: The Colleen Hoover adaptation the hosts still regret not seeing together; more McKenna Grace on the way.

films.title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple
films.note: January 16 release, framed as the first major studio movie to compete with Avatar for young male audiences after a clear month.

films.title: Wuthering Heights
films.note: Cited alongside 'Scream 7' as the February competition once Avatar's runway ends.

films.title: Scream 7
films.note: February release, part of the complicated post-Avatar stretch.

segments.heading: Awards season check-in
segments.note: National Society of Film Critics + Critics' Choice, before the Globes

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Won Best Picture at the National Society of Film Critics (57 points, in a landslide over 'Sinners' and 'The Secret Agent') and Critics' Choice, plus NYFCC, LAFCA, and NBR. Only Schindler's List, L.A. Confidential, and The Social Network previously swept those four. PTA won director at both.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: NSFC runner-up for picture; won cinematography (Autumn Durald Arkapaw), and at Critics' Choice took casting/ensemble (Francine Maisler), score, and young actor (Miles Caton). Coogler took original screenplay.

films.title: The Secret Agent
films.note: Kléber Mendonça Filho's film keeps winning: NSFC best film not in English (by one point) and Critics' Choice foreign language. A critics' movie by a former critic; Sean's takeaway of the weekend was how strong it is, with talk of Tânia Maria sneaking into supporting actress.

films.title: Blue Moon
films.note: Ethan Hawke won NSFC best actor; Robert Kaplow's script also cited. Richard Linklater tied for runner-up in director (with 'Nouvelle Vague').

films.title: Nouvelle Vague
films.note: Cited among Linklater's two films in the NSFC director runner-up spot.

films.title: Familiar Touch
films.note: Kathleen Chalfant won NSFC best actress (45 points), a classic critics'-body move.

films.title: Sentimental Value
films.note: Renate Reinsve was an NSFC actress runner-up; Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas placed in supporting actress; Stellan Skarsgard in supporting actor. The hosts think Lilleaas is coming.

films.title: Hamnet
films.note: Jessie Buckley's best-actress win is treated as the only truly locked race; her absence from the NSFC list read as an inevitability move. The film itself is a bit critically divisive.

films.title: Train Dreams
films.note: Adolpho Veloso won the Critics' Choice cinematography award and came second at NSFC; a live battle with 'Sinners.'

films.title: Frankenstein
films.note: Jacob Elordi won Critics' Choice supporting actor; the film also took production design, costumes, and hair/makeup. Evidence, the hosts say, that Normies really like Frankenstein; a lock for six-plus nominations.

films.title: Weapons
films.note: Amy Madigan's Critics' Choice supporting-actress win is the Marisa Tomei / Joan Plowright 'last chance' slot; the hosts don't think it changes the race but expect her nominated.

films.title: The Naked Gun
films.note: Won Critics' Choice best comedy, prompting a can-you-even-fill-out-five-comedies debate.

films.title: F1
films.note: Took Critics' Choice editing and sound; a strong below-the-line contender.

films.title: K-Pop Demon Hunters
films.note: Won Critics' Choice best animated feature; stay tuned.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
films.note: Won best stunt design (Wade Eastwood). Sean revisited it over the break and it didn't go well.

films.title: My Undesirable Friends: Part I - Last Air in Moscow
films.note: NSFC best non-fiction film and the pick to win the category, if voters will sit through five hours; the hosts think it's hard to turn away from.

segments.heading: The 26 most anticipated movies of 2026
segments.note: 13 apiece, Amanda then Sean, best last

picks.title: The Odyssey
picks.note: Her No. 1. Christopher Nolan puts every movie star in one film: Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Lupita, Zendaya, Charlize, Jon Bernthal, Benny Safdie, Mia Goth, and Robert Pattinson as Antinous. Blocking out July 14-24. Homer, undefeated for 3,000 years.

picks.title: Disclosure Day
picks.note: His No. 1, elevated by the December trailer and Universal's six-month runway. Likely the last major Spielberg blockbuster, an alien-invasion drama: Josh O'Connor, Emily Blunt, Colin Firth, Colman Domingo, Eve Hewson, Wyatt Russell. David Koepp scripts. A big-budget movie from a visionary director at scale, the kind Sean wants more of.

picks.title: Project Hail Mary
picks.note: Her No. 2. Ryan Gosling alone in space, from Lord & Miller and another Andy Weir novel. Amanda's seen the trailer 45,000 times; it doesn't feel as magical as the first, but if it's 'The Martian' reheated, she'll have a great time.

picks.title: Dune: Part Three
picks.note: His No. 2. Villeneuve's last villain movie before Bond, slated opposite 'Avengers: Doomsday' (one assumes Dune stands down, or bumps to Thanksgiving). Parts one and two were the rare spectacle IP that lived up to expectations; hopes for more Florence Pugh.

picks.title: Artificial
picks.note: Her No. 3, 'the real social reckoning.' Luca Guadagnino's Sam Altman movie, written by Simon Rich. Andrew Garfield as Altman in fleece vests (costumes reportedly by Jonathan Anderson), with Yura Borisov, Cooper Hoffman, Monica Barbaro. A coin-flip: could be great, could flop.

picks.title: The Adventures of Cliff Booth
picks.note: His No. 3, and a movie he's desperate to be good. David Fincher directs Tarantino's script, a quasi-sequel to 'Once Upon a Time in Hollywood' on Netflix. The novelization's Cliff is sillier and more freewheeling; Fincher's hands make the whole thing feel unusual. If good, it powers July.

picks.title: The Drama
picks.note: Her No. 4. Kristoffer Borgli directs Robert Pattinson and Zendaya through a fake engagement announcement in the Boston Globe. She loved the trailer, loves Zendaya, and rates Pattinson her No. 2 movie star.

picks.title: Jack of Spades
picks.note: His No. 4. Joel Coen solo, a gothic mystery shot in Scotland starring Josh O'Connor, Frances McDormand, Leslie Manville, Damian Lewis. Carter Burwell and Bruno Delbonnel back from 'The Tragedy of Macbeth.' Contemporary or period, no one's sure.

picks.title: Behemoth
picks.note: Her No. 5. Tony Gilroy writing and directing an original again, apparently about a cellist: Pedro Pascal, David Harbour, Ava Victor, Olivia Wilde, Searchlight. She trusts Gilroy every time.

picks.title: Paper Tiger
picks.note: His No. 5. James Gray back to his roots (echoes of 'Little Odessa'): two brothers, the American dream, a Russian-mafia scheme. Adam Driver, Scarlett Johansson, Miles Teller. Sean regrets leaving Gray off '25 for 25.'

picks.title: Possible Love
picks.note: Her No. 6, grabbed off Sean's international list. Lee Chang-dong's first feature since 'Burning' (eight years), a love quadrangle. Worth clearing a slot for on its own.

picks.title: Resident Evil
picks.note: His No. 6. Zach Cregger writing and directing the video-game adaptation and, it sounds, getting to do whatever he wants. Sean's in the bag for him; could be the best horror movie of the year.

picks.title: Narnia: The Magician's Nephew
picks.note: Her No. 7. She goes where Greta Gerwig goes, even to Narnia. Carey Mulligan, Emma Mackey, Daniel Craig (as Aslan?), music by Mark Ronson. Starting with the prequel that features Aslan's creation of the world.

picks.title: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma
picks.note: His No. 7. Jane Schoenbrunn's riff on a hardcore camp slasher, after 'We're All Going to the World's Fair' and 'I Saw the TV Glow.' Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson. Another serious director taking a swing at horror.

picks.title: Fjord
picks.note: Her No. 8, also lifted from Sean's international list. Cristian Mungiu (of 'R.M.N.') directs Sebastian Stan and Renate Reinsve as a couple ostracized in a remote Norwegian town, a spiritual sequel about alienation and immigration. Neon.

picks.title: Her Private Hell
picks.note: His No. 8. Nicolas Winding Refn returns to cinema (finally off streaming TV), 'an edgy, hypnotic, unhinged thriller with lots of glitter, sex, and violence.' Sophie Thatcher, Havana Rose Liu, Charles Melton, Diego Calva. A CR movie if there ever was one.

picks.title: The Moment
picks.note: Her No. 9. The Charli XCX A24 feature; the pick that launched a full Charli XCX Letterboxd holiday report. January 30 release.

picks.title: October
picks.note: His No. 9. Jeremy Saulnier back to the horror-action of 'Blue Ruin' and 'Green Room' for A24: Corey Michael Smith, Chase Sui Wonders, Sophie Wilde. Sidecar pick: 'Enemies,' Henry Dunham's Ari Aster-produced A24 thriller with Jeremy Allen White and Austin Butler. He needs A24 to get evil again.

picks.title: How to Make a Killing
picks.note: Her No. 10. John Patton Ford's follow-up to 'Emily the Criminal,' a soft riff on 'Kind Hearts and Coronets': Glen Powell disinherited and taking revenge on his family, with Margaret Qualley. Maybe Powell wants to be Alec Guinness.

picks.title: Remain
picks.note: His No. 10, a complete emotional unknown. M. Night Shyamalan directs Jake Gyllenhaal as a depressed Cape Cod architect, opposite Phoebe Dynevor; story by Nicholas Sparks. Shot on VistaVision, October 23 ghost story.

picks.title: The Entertainment System Is Down
picks.note: Her No. 11. Ruben Ostlund's plane satire (the follow-up to 'Triangle of Sadness') with Keanu Reeves, Daniel Bruhl, Samantha Morton, Nicholas Braun, and, most important, Kirsten Dunst. She goes where Kirsten goes.

picks.title: Digger
picks.note: His No. 11. Alejandro Gonzalez Inarritu directs Tom Cruise in an original film, which barely happens anymore. Sean's not a huge Inarritu fan and hopes it isn't too goofy; likely a Venice premiere. He's really here for what Cruise can do.

picks.title: The Rip
picks.note: Her No. 12. Joe Carnahan's Miami narcotics-heist movie with Ben Affleck and Matt Damon staring at each other deciding if they're good guys or bad guys. Netflix, January 16. Moms and dads can have fun too.

picks.title: Primetime
picks.note: His No. 12. Lance Oppenheim's first narrative feature (A24), set in the 'To Catch a Predator' world with Robert Pattinson as a Chris Hansen-like figure. Sidecar: 'Ink,' Danny Boyle's younger-Rupert-Murdoch film with Guy Pearce, Jack O'Connell, and Claire Foy.

picks.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2
picks.note: Her No. 13, a three-film tie of beloved things being reheated. 'The most rewatchable movie of all time' (salute, Bill Simmons). She just needs it not to be embarrassing.

picks.title: The Social Reckoning
picks.note: Part of her No. 13 tie. Aaron Sorkin writing and directing a Facebook movie without Jesse Eisenberg's Zuckerberg, following her No. 1 on '25 for 25.' Two Jeremys and a Mikey. She's nervous but doubts it can sully the original.

picks.title: Sense and Sensibility
picks.note: The last of her No. 13 tie. Georgia Oakley directs Daisy Edgar-Jones as Elinor, Esmé Creed-Miles as Marianne, Caitríona Balfe as Mrs. Dashwood, George MacKay as Edward. The Emma Thompson version is a cortex for her.

picks.title: The Bride
picks.note: His No. 13, sharing Amanda's unease. Maggie Gyllenhaal's follow-up to 'The Lost Daughter': Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale in a black-and-white 'Bride of Frankenstein' riff. Put off by the trailer and the bad production scuttlebutt; it's in the 'Mickey 17' spot at the same Warner Bros.

segments.heading: Honorable mentions
segments.note: the international slate and the deep cuts

picks.title: Enemies
picks.note: Henry Dunham's follow-up to 'Standoff at Sparrow Creek,' produced by Ari Aster for A24: a detective's battle of wits with a fugitive contract killer. Jeremy Allen White, Austin Butler, Anna Sawai.

picks.title: Ink
picks.note: Danny Boyle's film about a younger Rupert Murdoch, played by Guy Pearce, with Jack O'Connell and Claire Foy. Studiocanal; started shooting in October, so maybe 2026. Boyle's born to make sharp biopic snapshots (see 'Steve Jobs').

picks.title: Hope
picks.note: Na Hong-jin's first film since 'The Wailing.' Sean won't spoil the logline; he thinks it's out this year.

picks.title: Out of This World
picks.note: The new Albert Serra feature, after the bullfighting doc 'Afternoons of Solitude' and 'Pacifiction.'

picks.title: Parallel Tales
picks.note: A new Asghar Farhadi movie.

picks.title: Coward
picks.note: Lukas Dhont's follow-up to 'Close.'

picks.title: Butterfly Jam
picks.note: The new Kantemir Balagov film.

picks.title: Pressure
picks.note: A World War II movie told from the meteorologist's chair: Andrew Scott gives Eisenhower (Brendan Fraser) the D-Day weather report. Kerry Condon co-stars.

picks.title: A Long Winter
picks.note: Andrew Haigh's new film, from a Colm Tóibín short story, with Evan Moss-Bachrach. Sounds like people sad in winter, which, same.

picks.title: Verity
picks.note: Another Colleen Hoover adaptation, directed by Michael Showalter, with Dakota Johnson, Anne Hathaway, Josh Hartnett. Looks insane; if we're doing this, really do it.

picks.title: Panic Carefully
picks.note: Sam Esmail's undated Warner Bros. movie with Julia Roberts; feels like it's happening this year.

picks.title: The Basics of Philosophy
picks.note: The new Paul Schrader movie. Come on, bro.

picks.title: Frost Rider
picks.note: A J.J. Abrams movie starring Glen Powell. Open to it.

picks.title: Bucking Fastard
picks.note: A new Werner Herzog movie about twins, played by Rooney Mara and Kate Mara. Probably Telluride.

picks.title: Here Comes the Flood
picks.note: Fernando Meirelles' Netflix con thriller with Denzel Washington, Robert Pattinson, Daisy Edgar-Jones. A bank guard, a teller, and a master thief in a game of double-crosses.

picks.title: Flowervale Street
picks.note: David Robert Mitchell's dinosaur sci-fi with Anne Hathaway, anticipated for 47 years.

picks.title: The History of Concrete
picks.note: A documentary premiering at Sundance from John Wilson, of the beloved HBO series.

picks.title: Wild Horse Nine
picks.note: A Martin McDonagh movie, reportedly with Sam Rockwell; a 'Seven Psychopaths' plus 'Three Billboards' reunion.

picks.title: I Love Boosters
picks.note: A new Boots Riley movie.

picks.title: The Dog Stars
picks.note: Ridley Scott's film, moved from March to October, with Jacob Elordi, Margaret Qualley, Josh Brolin, Guy Pearce, Benedict Wong. Money in them hills.

picks.title: Wildwood
picks.note: Another Travis Knight movie, a Laika stop-motion feature.

picks.title: Ray Gunn
picks.note: Brad Bird's long-gestating animated film, finally happening at Netflix.

films.title: Obsession
films.note: Curry Barker's film, which played festivals in the fall and was picked up by Focus; thought to be great.

films.title: Send Help
films.note: Sam Raimi's new movie, out January 30 (something for everyone that day).

films.title: Evil Dead Burn
films.note: The sixth 'Evil Dead' film; could be good.

films.title: Other Mommy
films.note: A horror movie starring Jessica Chastain, directed by Rob Savage ('Host'). Amanda was disappointed it's a horror movie and not just a movie about her life.

films.title: The Mummy
films.note: A new version of the Mummy, coming soon.

films.title: Undertow
films.note: A very small-budget A24 horror movie Sean has already seen, thought to be playing Sundance.

films.title: Backrooms
films.note: Kane Parsons' creepypasta adaptation for A24. Really good teaser.

films.title: Victorian Psycho
films.note: Cited (a.k.a. Amanda Dobbins, per Sean); she's more of an Elizabethan psycho.

films.title: Terrifier 4
films.note: Now is the time to get on board, after 'Terrifier 3.'

films.title: The Land of Nod
films.note: Kyle Edward Ball's follow-up to 'Skinamarink' (Sean's favorite film of 2022) for A24. Looks really interesting.

films.title: Scary Movie 6
films.note: Cited in the horror-wave rundown.

films.title: People We Meet on Vacation
films.note: The week's Emily Henry adaptation, on Netflix, with Emily Bader and Tom Blythe. Henry writes rom-coms that keep going (lots of sex, then love).

films.title: One Night Only
films.note: A Will Gluck movie ('Easy A,' 'Friends with Benefits') with Callum Turner and Monica Barbaro. Molly Ringwald, LeVar Burton, Maya Hawke, Julia Fox, Este Haim. Original 'Father of the Bride' or Nancy Meyers?

films.title: Ladies First
films.note: A Netflix black-and-white romantic comedy loosely inspired by the French film 'I Am Not an Easy Man': a male chauvinist in an alternate world of reversed power dynamics. Sacha Baron Cohen, Rosamund Pike, Richard E. Grant, Fiona Shaw.

films.title: Power Ballad
films.note: A John Carney movie (June) with Paul Rudd and Nick Jonas, about an aging rock star. Sean saw an extended CinemaCon teaser; looked fine. 'Once' and 'Sing Street' are great; the rest, mixed.

films.title: The Invite
films.note: Olivia Wilde directs Seth Rogen, Penélope Cruz, Edward Norton: a couple in a rough patch gets invited to the neighbors' weekly orgies. A swinger comedy. Huge year for Wilde.

films.title: I Want Your Sex
films.note: Gregg Araki's new movie with Olivia Wilde opposite Cooper Hoffman.

segments.heading: The big IP slate
segments.note: the biggest movies of the year, promised reviews and all

films.title: Avengers: Doomsday
films.note: Robert Downey Jr. back as Doctor Doom (of Latveria), with the Fox X-Men (James Marsden, Patrick Stewart), Thor, Shuri. Sean is bored of being disappointed and shitting on these; he wants it to be fun. Not even all the Avengers movies are good ('Age of Ultron' isn't).

films.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
films.note: Peter starts over: friends off to college and made to forget him, MJ moved on. John Bernthal as the Punisher, Mark Ruffalo's Hulk, Michael Mando as Scorpion. Amanda loved 'No Way Home' and wept.

films.title: Toy Story 5
films.note: Here comes the iPad: a character named Lily Pad, a frog-themed smart tablet representing modern screen time. Honestly a good idea.

films.title: Supergirl
films.note: One of two DC (just-U) movies cited.

films.title: Clayface
films.note: A failed actor turns to crime, disfigured and able to look like anyone. Sean's in.

films.title: Michael
films.note: The Michael Jackson biopic, bumped out of the holidays, now April.

films.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu
films.note: Mentioned 45 times; Amanda's son is deep in the Grogu of it all.

films.title: Moana
films.note: The live-action version; Amanda introduced the animated one to her kids at Thanksgiving.

films.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
films.note: April release, following 'The Super Mario Bros. Movie' (an A-plus hit in Amanda's house). Rosalina voiced by Brie Larson.

films.title: Masters of the Universe
films.note: Travis Knight ('Bumblebee') directs Amazon's $100M He-Man, all practical effects: Nicholas Galitzine as He-Man, Jared Leto as Skeletor, Idris Elba as Man-At-Arms, Camila Mendes as Teela. Sean's childhood; Amanda felt like an alien.

films.title: Mortal Kombat 2
films.note: A sequel to the too-serious first film; part of the arch, agro gag-comedy IP energy with Masters of the Universe and Street Fighter.

films.title: Street Fighter
films.note: Cited alongside 'Mortal Kombat 2' and 'Masters of the Universe' as the is-this-serious-or-not stunt-casting trio.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping
films.note: In the big-IP rundown.

films.title: Hoppers
films.note: The new Pixar movie; Amanda's son seemed interested.

films.title: Minions 3
films.note: Your box office right there, alongside 'The Odyssey' and 'Dune 3.'

segments.heading: Is this really happening?
segments.note: the maybe-2026 movies nobody can confirm

films.title: The Way of the Wind
films.note: Terrence Malick's long-shot story of Jesus Christ, shot years ago, with Géza Röhrig ('Marty Supreme') as Jesus. Some say they've seen parts; no one really knows.

films.title: Horizon: An American Saga - Chapter 2
films.note: Screened at Santa Barbara 14 months ago and then vanished; Griffin Newman went and enjoyed it. Definitely made, latest unknown.

films.title: Whitney Springs
films.note: The Trey Parker / Matt Stone / Kendrick Lamar movie, dated twice by Paramount and now off the schedule. A musical? A movie about slavery? Nobody knows.

films.title: Cry to Heaven
films.note: Tom Ford's adaptation of Anne Rice's novel about 18th-century Italian opera and the castrati: Nicholas Hoult, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Owen Cooper, allegedly Adele. Sounds very Tom Ford.
